what_it_does: This file is a structured extraction and design-system ledger of a 17-slide investor pitch deck for ConceptV, cataloging the deck's narrative arc, attention flow, visual language, layout modes, components, typography, color palette, geometry, spacing, and content budgets as a set of findings, generator rules, and reusable components.
observations: The deck is described as a 17-slide 'pitch-deck' with a deliberate problem→solution→proof→commercialise→invest narrative arc.

Findings are grouped into categories such as gradient-shading-opacity, geometry, type-hierarchy, padding-alignment, color-palette, atom, line-connector, texture, chrome-mode, zone, sequence, attention-direction, emergent-interactivity, and emergent-content-budget.
Each finding includes a name, recipe, evidence, emergent flag, and generator rule, making the file prescriptive as well as descriptive.
A persistent V-circle badge and a bottom diagonal-stripe bar are mandated on every non-photo slide.
Specific shape semantics are enforced: hexagon = brand-core system node, diamond = Vi identity, circle = person/concept nodes, rounded rectangle = tags/buttons/timeline nodes.

Components include HexNetworkDiagram, StatMetricStrip, ComparisonPanel, PropertyStageIconRow, ProfileCard, RoundedTagCluster, CTAButtonBar, TimelineCascadeTree, and DiagonalStripeBar, each with reuse notes.
Content samples provide character, word, and line budgets for titles, bullets, captions, metrics, buttons, tags, and labels.
The file distinguishes deck chrome (warm cream, light document mode) from product UI chrome (dark header bars) shown inside screenshots.
standouts: The deck is treated as a narrative funnel where layout-mode shifts signal phase changes rather than using section-break slides.
Golden chevron bullets are used as a vertical eye-guide rail in copy zones.
A mandatory 10–14px diagonal gold-and-white stripe bar runs along the bottom edge of every non-photo slide, visually quoting the floor texture from the cover render.
Strict content budgets are tied to computed zone widths, with non-negotiable single-line constraints for subheadings, buttons, and tags.
The hexagon shape is reserved exclusively for brand-core system nodes, while the diamond is reserved for the Vi identity.
conventions: Follows a tokenized design-system style with explicit color, spacing, type-size, and shape rules.
Uses evidence + recipe + generator_rule structure consistently across findings.
Separates descriptive documentation from prescriptive reuse guidance in components.
Maintains a warm cream ground, charcoal text hierarchy, and golden accent palette throughout.
Does not break its own conventions; it is internally consistent in categorization and rule phrasing.
concerns: Many values are approximate or eyeballed (e.g., '#C8960A approx', '~5–7% opacity', 'approximately 32–36px') rather than measured from a source asset.
There is no explicit reference to the original pitch-deck file, image assets, or extraction method.
The 'emergent' flag is not defined, so its basis is unclear from the file alone.
Some generator rules are highly prescriptive but lack validation data or fallback guidance.
Content samples may be synthetic or reconstructed rather than verbatim slide text.
notes: A dense, rule-based visual-language extraction of a ConceptV investor pitch deck, intended to be used as a reproducible design substrate.
questions: What is the original source artifact (PDF, images, Figma file) from which this extraction was made?
Were color, spacing, and opacity values measured programmatically or estimated visually?
How was the 'emergent' boolean determined for each finding?
Are the content_samples actual deck text or reconstructed placeholders?
What tooling or process produced this extracted JSON?
How does this file relate to other files in the counterpart-design repository?
purpose_vs_actual: The file does not contain an explicit 'purpose' field, but its path and content indicate it is an ingested extraction of a pitch deck's design system; its actual content matches that inferred purpose closely.
apparent_themes: Narrative-as-layout: phase changes are communicated through layout-mode shifts.
Visual rhetoric: problem-state visuals are chaotic, solution-state visuals are ordered.
Tokenized visual system with strict shape semantics.
Computed proportional spacing and zone-based content budgets.
Aspirational framing through warm photorealistic renders at open and close.
Brand-gold discipline and avoidance of pure black or pure white.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to convert a visual pitch-deck artifact into a machine-readable generative rule set, so the deck's look, feel, and narrative structure can be audited, reproduced, or fused with other design substrates.
novelty: It treats a pitch deck as a code/design substrate with granular generative rules such as 'border-radius encodes interaction level', 'dashed lines indicate future/potential connections', and 'text budgets are zone-width-relative', which is unusually systematic for a static-deck extraction.
contribution: This file contributes a comprehensive, categorized visual-language specification for a warm-cream, gold-accented investor pitch deck, including reusable components, shape semantics, typography and spacing tokens, color rules, narrative arc, attention-flow annotations, and content-budget samples. It preserves the relationship between layout mode and narrative phase, and the deliberate contrast between deck chrome and product UI chrome.
summary_for_embedding: A structured extraction of a 17-slide ConceptV investor pitch deck documenting its visual design system, narrative arc, layout modes, components, color/typography/spacing tokens, shape semantics, and content budgets as generative rules and reusable patterns.
intention_for_embedding: To capture the pitch deck's visual DNA as a machine-readable design substrate for reproduction, review, or fusion with other code/design substrates.
